summary: Joel Spolsky announces the launch of the Fog Creek Software Management Training Program, a three-year intensive program designed to develop the next generation of software management talent. The post acknowledges that Fog Creek has primarily hired programmers up to this point and signals a strategic shift toward building management capacity. The program aims to teach participants everything Fog Creek knows about the software industry.
key_points: Fog Creek recognized a gap in their hiring pipeline: they had focused almost exclusively on programmers and needed to cultivate management talent
The management training program is a three-year intensive commitment, signaling serious investment in developing leaders rather than just hiring them
The program reflects Spolsky's belief that software management is a distinct skill that can and should be systematically taught
money_quotes: Until now, most of our hires have been programmers. That's a start, but we need to start hiring the next generation of management too.
key_insight: Great software companies need to invest in developing management talent with the same intentionality they apply to hiring great programmers.
